questions.question: What did the 2025 meta-analysis of 20 RCTs find regarding vitamin D supplementation and depression?

options.text: No significant effect was found
A significant reduction in depressive symptoms with SMD = -0.36 and p < 0.00001
Vitamin D only helped people under age 30
Vitamin D worsened depressive symptoms in most participants

questions.explanation: The 2025 meta-analysis of 20 RCTs found a small-to-moderate but highly significant effect: SMD = -0.36 with p < 0.00001. The effect was moderated by baseline vitamin D status, dose (2000+ IU/day more effective), and duration (8+ weeks). This is the most definitive evidence to date.

questions.question: What was the paradoxical finding in the 2023 vitamin D meta-analysis?

options.text: Vitamin D supplementation caused anxiety in all participants
Only vitamin D2 was effective, not D3
Participants with adequate baseline vitamin D levels showed a stronger antidepressant response than those with deficient levels
Men responded to vitamin D supplementation but women did not

questions.explanation: The 2023 meta-analysis found that participants with adequate baseline vitamin D (>50 nmol/L) showed a paradoxically stronger antidepressant response to supplementation than those with deficient levels. This may be because those starting from adequate levels reach the optimal therapeutic range (75-100 nmol/L) more quickly, or because chronic deficiency downregulates vitamin D receptors.

questions.question: Why should vitamin K2 (MK-7) be co-supplemented with high-dose vitamin D?

options.text: K2 increases the absorption of vitamin D in the gut
K2 activates proteins that direct calcium to bones and prevent arterial calcification, since vitamin D increases calcium absorption
K2 prevents vitamin D from being converted to its active form too quickly
K2 is required to prevent vitamin D from causing skin rashes

questions.explanation: Vitamin D increases calcium absorption from the gut. K2 activates two proteins — osteocalcin (directs calcium to bone) and matrix Gla-protein (prevents arterial calcification) — that ensure calcium goes where it is needed. Without K2, increased calcium from vitamin D can deposit in arteries and soft tissues.

questions.question: What is the optimal serum 25(OH)D range for mental health, according to the lesson?
questions.explanation: The optimal range for mental health is 75-100 nmol/L (30-40 ng/mL). Below 50 nmol/L is considered deficiency, while 50-75 nmol/L is insufficiency. Above 150 nmol/L is potentially excessive, and above 375 nmol/L carries toxicity risk.

questions.question: Why is magnesium considered essential for vitamin D supplementation to be effective?

options.text: Magnesium prevents vitamin D from being excreted too quickly
Magnesium is required for the conversion of vitamin D3 through its metabolic pathway to the active form, 1,25(OH)2D
Magnesium makes vitamin D taste better when taken as a supplement
Magnesium and vitamin D must always be taken at exactly the same time

questions.explanation: The pathway from supplemental vitamin D3 to 25(OH)D to 1,25(OH)2D (the active form) requires adequate magnesium. Without addressing magnesium, vitamin D supplementation may produce suboptimal metabolite conversion. This is one reason the 2023 paradoxical finding may have occurred — magnesium deficiency frequently co-occurs with vitamin D deficiency.

title: Vitamin D: Testing, Supplementation, and Mental Health - Quiz
